message.content:
You are an expert NestJS developer implementing RECO-806.

Working directory: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806
ALL commands must run with: cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && <command>

TASK: Fix 3 ESLint warnings in `src/modules/rest/related-products/related-products.controller.ts`:
- Line 42: `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` — unnecessary `?.` and `??` on non-nullish responseFormatter values

STEP 1: Read the controller file around line 42 to understand the pattern.

STEP 2: Read the response DTO to understand the types (check src/dto/related-products/ or src/modules/rest/related-products/).

STEP 3: Fix the warnings. The responseFormatter lambda accesses properties like `res?.brand_products?.length ?? 0`. If the DTO types guarantee these are non-nullable, remove the `?.` and `??`. 

IMPORTANT: Check if the response can actually be undefined/null at runtime (e.g., if the service returns undefined on error). If defensive code IS needed at runtime despite TypeScript types, use `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` instead of removing the guards.

Look at the EXISTING pattern on this branch — commit `9817ba5` added eslint-disable comments to the FBT controller for exactly this reason. Check:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && git show 9817ba5 --stat
```
and read how that commit handled the same pattern.

STEP 4: Run lint to verify 0 warnings:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && npx eslint src/modules/rest/related-products/related-products.controller.ts
```

STEP 5: Run type-check:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && npx tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -10
```

STEP 6: Run unit tests:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && npx dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --config ./test/jest-unit.config.ts 2>&1 | tail -10
```

STEP 7: Copy .env.test from main repo if missing:
```bash
cp /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.env.test /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806/.env.test 2>/dev/null || true
```

STEP 8: Commit:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "RECO-806: fix ESLint warnings in related-products.controller.ts

Remove unnecessary optional chain and nullish coalescing in responseFormatter,
or add eslint-disable if defensive code is needed at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

STEP 9: Push:
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806 && git push --no-verify -u origin RECO-806
```

NO COMMENTS in code except ESLint disable directives.
Report: what approach you chose (remove guards vs eslint-disable), files changed, test results.

content.text: I'll work through this systematically. Let me start by reading the controller and understanding the existing pattern.

input.description: Check the FBT controller commit that handled the same pattern

content.content:
commit 9817ba509dde601bc9d27da11ebd10ae4a356172
Author: 4thwithme <aopopenko@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Apr 3 13:53:06 2026 +0200

    RECO-802: restore defensive ?./?? in responseFormatter with eslint-disable
    
    The responseFormatter callbacks in @LogExecution receive untyped runtime
    values (cast via `as TResponse`), so optional chaining and nullish
    coalescing are needed for safety even though the DTO types are non-optional.
    Added eslint-disable comments to suppress the no-unnecessary-condition warnings.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 .../frequently-bought-together.controller.ts              | 15 +++++++++------
 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

content.text:
The pattern is clear. The FBT controller used `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` on the line before each `responseFormatter` arrow function. Now I need to apply the same pattern to the related-products controller. The `brand_products` property on the DTO is non-optional (`brand_products: IRelatedProduct[]`), but since `@LogExecution` casts via `as TResponse` at runtime, the defensive `?.` and `??` are still needed.

Let me apply the fix:

content.content: The file /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806/src/modules/rest/related-products/related-products.controller.ts has been updated successfully.

  43:14  warning  Unnecessary optional chain on a non-nullish value                                                               @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition
  43:30  warning  Unnecessary optional chain on a non-nullish value                                                               @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition

content.text: The eslint-disable is not being picked up on the right line. The issue is that the `// eslint-disable-next-line` needs to be on the line immediately before line 43 (the `count:` line), not before the `responseFormatter:` line. Let me check the current state of the file:

content.text:
The eslint-disable is being placed on the `responseFormatter:` line (42) but the actual violations are on line 43. The FBT pattern works because the formatter is a single-line arrow function on the same line as `responseFormatter:`. But here the body `({ count: res?.brand_products?.length ?? 0 })` is on line 43.

The issue is the eslint-disable-next-line only disables for the very next line. I need to place the comment on line 42 to cover line 43. Wait — it IS on line 41, covering line 42, not line 43. I need to move it so it covers line 43.

The cleanest approach: collapse the responseFormatter to a single line (like the FBT pattern), then the eslint-disable-next-line on the responseFormatter line will cover the violations inline.

Let me restructure so the entire responseFormatter including the body is on one line, matching the FBT pattern exactly:

content.text:
Done. Here's the summary:

**Approach chosen: `eslint-disable-next-line` (same as FBT controller in commit 9817ba5)**

The `@LogExecution` decorator casts the runtime response value via `as TResponse`, meaning the responseFormatter lambda can receive `undefined` at runtime even though the DTO types declare `brand_products` as non-optional. The defensive `?.` and `??` are genuinely needed for runtime safety, so removing them would be wrong.

**Fix applied:**

File: `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service-RECO-806/src/modules/rest/related-products/related-products.controller.ts`

Added `// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` on the line immediately before `count: res?.brand_products?.length ?? 0` inside the `getBrandProducts` responseFormatter. This matches the exact pattern established in the FBT controller.

**Results:**
- ESLint: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- TypeScript: no type errors
- Unit tests: 1028 passed, 37 suites
- Branch pushed to `origin/RECO-806`
